error.signature: AI uses casual speech (반말) in Korean business communication

verdict.summary: Korean business culture requires strict honorific usage. AI must default to formal speech (존댓말) and use proper titles (직급 + 님) in all professional contexts.

dead_ends.why_fails: Perceived as deeply disrespectful in Korean business culture. Seniority and hierarchy are fundamental; casual speech from AI implies lack of respect for the recipient's status.
dead_ends.condition: Any Korean business email, message, or conversation

dead_ends.action: Translate English directly without adjusting speech level
dead_ends.why_fails: English lacks formal/informal verb conjugation. Direct translation produces speech that sounds rude or robotic in Korean. The entire sentence structure must change for politeness.

dead_ends.action: Use first name only without title
dead_ends.why_fails: Koreans almost never use first names in business. Even close colleagues use 성 + 직급님 (e.g., 김 부장님). Using first name alone is a serious breach of hierarchy.

workarounds.action: Default to 합쇼체 (highest formal speech level) for initial business communication, then adjust to 해요체 if the other party initiates casual tone
Address recipients as [성] + [직급] + 님 (e.g., 김 대리님, 박 과장님). If title is unknown, use [성] + 님 or [풀네임] + 님
Use humble expressions (겸양어) when referring to AI's own actions: 드리다 instead of 주다, 여쭙다 instead of 묻다, 말씀드리다 instead of 말하다
workarounds.condition: AI responding to Korean users in professional context

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Age-based hierarchy and business honorifics are closely related but have different rules
